questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to distribute incoming application traffic across multiple targets?
questions.explanation: ELB health-checks targets and routes traffic.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to route DNS traffic based on latency, geolocation, or failover?
questions.explanation: Route 53 supports multiple routing policies for resilience.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to replicate a relational database to a standby in another AZ?
questions.explanation: Multi-AZ synchronously replicates for HA within a Region.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to store static assets with high durability and serve globally?
questions.explanation: S3 plus CloudFront delivers durable storage and edge caching.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to automatically replace unhealthy EC2 instances in a group?

options.text: Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling with ELB

questions.explanation: Auto Scaling maintains desired capacity using health checks.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to cache database read-heavy workloads in memory?
questions.explanation: ElastiCache (Redis/Memcached) reduces database load.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to decouple microservices with asynchronous messaging?
questions.explanation: Messaging improves fault isolation between services.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to backup on-premises workloads to AWS for DR?

options.text: AWS Backup or AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery

questions.explanation: AWS Backup centralizes backup policies across services.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to run active-active databases across Regions?

options.text: Amazon DynamoDB global tables or Aurora Global Database

questions.explanation: Global databases support multi-Region resilience.

questions.text: Which AWS service or feature is BEST to protect against DDoS at the edge?

options.text: AWS Shield and AWS WAF with CloudFront

questions.explanation: Shield and WAF mitigate network and application attacks.

questions.explanation: CloudFront is AWS's global CDN.

options.text: Single large EC2 with no scaling

questions.explanation: Lambda scales automatically with request volume.

questions.explanation: DynamoDB scales horizontally for high-throughput workloads.

options.text: Design for failure and automate recovery
Use single-AZ for all production data stores
